Adrian Oliver — adrianoliver.dev
Full-Stack Developer · E-Commerce & Inventory Systems
About
Adrian Oliver is a full-stack developer specialized in
building e-commerce platforms and inventory management
systems for apparel and retail brands.
Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL,
Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, Resend, WhatsApp Meta API.
Available for international remote projects.
Contact: hello@adrianoliver.dev
Profiles

Portfolio: https://adrianoliver.dev
GitHub: https://github.com/adrianoliver-dev
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/adrianoliver-dev
X: https://x.com/adrianoliver_
Upwork: https://www.upwork.com/freelancers/~0144ace41cb9797f02

Projects
Lukess Home — Omnichannel E-Commerce Platform
URL: https://adrianoliver.dev/projects/lukess-home
Live: https://store.adrianoliver.dev
Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, Resend, WhatsApp Meta API
Description: Full e-commerce system for a 4-location clothing
retailer. Atomic PostgreSQL triggers preventing overselling
across all locations simultaneously. 3 checkout methods
(Stripe, QR bank transfer, cash pickup). 10 email templates
via Resend. 9 WhatsApp Meta API approved templates.
Real-time inventory sync. 99/100 PageSpeed desktop.
Lukess Inventory — Custom ERP + POS System
URL: https://adrianoliver.dev/projects/lukess-inventory-system
Live: https://inventory.adrianoliver.dev
Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, PostgreSQL, Recharts
Description: Enterprise ERP and POS for multi-location retail.
19-table PostgreSQL schema with row-level security. 7-state
order machine with automated email and WhatsApp notifications.
3 roles (admin, manager, staff) enforced at the database level
via RLS and Next.js Edge middleware. Recharts dashboards
exportable to PDF and Excel. Immutable audit trail.
Solnr Studio — Luxury E-Commerce (In Development)
URL: https://adrianoliver.dev/projects/solnr-studio
Description: Quiet luxury menswear e-commerce showcase.
Editorial catalog, animated cart drawer, dark-mode-first
design system. Stack: Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind CSS v4,
Framer Motion.
Blog Posts
How We Replaced 3 Spreadsheets with a Real-Time Inventory System
URL: https://adrianoliver.dev/blog/inventory-system-retail
Published: 2026-03-13
Tags: Inventory Management, Retail Systems, Supabase, PostgreSQL
Summary: A 4-location clothing retailer replaced manual
spreadsheets with a custom ERP built on PostgreSQL triggers
that prevent overselling at the database level.
Next.js vs Shopify for Apparel Brands: An Honest Comparison
URL: https://adrianoliver.dev/blog/nextjs-vs-shopify-apparel
Published: 2026-03-17
Tags: Next.js, Shopify, E-Commerce, Architecture
Summary: When a fully custom Next.js system beats Shopify
for apparel brands — and when it doesn't. Covers multi-location
inventory, performance, data ownership, and total cost.
5 Checkout UX Mistakes That Kill Apparel E-Commerce Conversions
URL: https://adrianoliver.dev/blog/ecommerce-checkout-ux
Published: 2026-03-20
Tags: E-Commerce, UX, Checkout, Conversion
Summary: The five most common checkout mistakes in apparel
e-commerce and how to fix them: forced registration, missing
size guides, too many form fields, hidden shipping costs,
and payment method mismatch.
Services

Custom e-commerce development (Next.js + Supabase)
Inventory and POS systems for multi-location retail
Business web applications with real-time data and RBAC
Technical consulting for retail and apparel brands
